chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Appln. No.D-71 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. 1. For orders on M.A. No.602/2025 2. For orders on M.A. No.603/2025. 3. For hearing of bail application. 03.09.2025 Mr. Shoukat Ali Bohio, Advocate alongwith applicants. ******** 1. Urgency allowed. 2. Granted, subject to all just exceptions. 3. Through instant bail application, applicants Naveed Ali, Allah Dad, Ajeeb, Dilber, Ameer Bux, Khan Muhammad, Ghulam Sarwar, Ashique Ali, Barkat Ali, Sadiq Ali, Jan Muhammad, Shah Nawaz, Ghulam Qadir and Aslam seek protective bail in Crime No.626/2023 registered at Police Station, Moro for offence under Sections 302, 324, 114, 149, 337H PPC 6/7 ATA. It is, inter alia, contended that applicants are innocent and have falsely been involved in this case by the Police with malafide intention and ulterior motives. It is further contended that as soon as applicants came to know they tried to surrender themselves before the concerned Court for pre-arrest but applicants have grave apprehension of their being arrest before reaching the same at the hands of concerned police hence, they have approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable them to appear before concerned Court they are granted protective bail for a period of Five days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each (Rupees thirty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Meanwhile operation of NBWs, if any, issued against the applicants is suspended for the aforesaid period.

Be that as it may, without touching merits and demerits of the case and in order to enable them to appear before concerned Court they are granted protective bail for a period of Five days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- each (Rupees thirty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. Meanwhile operation of NBWs, if any, issued against the applicants is suspended for the aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicants before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicants fail to surrender before the concerned Court within stipulated period, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Ihsan/PS
